rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Re: Waiver Of Attorney-Client Privilege
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF LAURA CONTRERAS' Motion Re: Waiver Of Attorney-Client Privilege DENIED DENIED without prejudice to be raised at trial. = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Against Defts Curtis Dowling And Dowling & Marquez Llp
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF LAURA CONTRERAS Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants Curtis Dowling And Dowling & Marquez Llp GRANTED. Defendants' motion under CCP 425.16 was filed in violation of CCP 128.7(c)(1). Plaintiffs are correct that under the existing authorities, law of the case and allegations in the complaint, aiding and abetting an illegal entry is not protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16. Total amout of sanctions is $8,010.00 = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To Strike 4th Amended Complaint Filed On Feb 15, 2013
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS CURTIS DOWLING, BECKMAN, MARQUEZ & DOWLING LLP's Re-Notice Of Motion To Strike 4th Amended Complaint Filed On February 15, 2013 DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the sole cause of action alleged against the moving defendant arises out of protected activity. Contrary to moving party's assertions, complaint as drafted charges defendants not with whatever advice (not mentioned in the complaint) defendants may have given to Butterworths and Stuart, but with the actual conduct of the Butterworths and Stuart in breaking into Contreras's unit. Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Limited (1994) 7 al.4th 503, 510. Such conduct has already been determined by the Court of Appeal twice not to arise out of protected activity. Allegations regarding moving defendant's statements after the alleged entries that (1) the first alleged entry was not illegal; and (2) the second alleged entry never happened do not form the basis of liability, but are offered as evidence of the course of conduct forming the basis of liability. (See Action Apartment Assoc. v. City of Santa Monica (2007) 41 Cal.4th 1232, 1248.) Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471 is distinguishable and inapplicable to the facts and allegations at bar. Additionally, Plaintiff provided sufficient evidence under the second prong re: tenant harassment, aiding and abetting. Defendants' counsel is sanctioned $900 to be paid to the Court within 10 days. Defendants' counsel after originally filing this motion in improper department with full knowledge of such impropriety (See March 27, 2014 order), failed to comply with the Court's order and deliver courtesy copies to department 501 per May 7, 2014 order. = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Demurrer To Fourth Amended Filed Feb 15, 13
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT CURTIS DOWLING, BECKMAN, MARQUEZ & DOWLING LLP, Re-Notice Of Demurrer To Fourth Amended Filed February 15, 2013 OVERRULED, in addition to any theory of liability based on conspiracy, plaintiff's claims against moving defendants are based on aiding and abetting. Aiding and abetting is not addressed in the notice of motion and the moving papers. = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Pltf Laura Contreras To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF LAURA CONTRERAS' Motion To Compel And For Sanctions OFF CALENDAR. Discovery stay per CCP 425.16(g). No authority cited for the proposition that the stay does not extend to the filing of the motion and covers hearing of the motion. = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, line 7. Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is timely in light of the stay on appeal and the original trial date being vacated due to the stay. Motion is GRANTED as to the Request for Production No. 16 to Posting Revenue Access, LLC and No. 17 to Hicks limited to the property addresses and contact information of adjacent property owners held in defendants' database. Responses shall be provided within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Motion is other wise denied. Requests for sanctions are denied. = (501/PHA) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BORN VS. MARIA GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue;
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANTS MARIA GONZALEZ, LILA GONZALEZ-GARFINKEL's Motion To Transfer Venue; DENIED. Convenience of defendant's witnesses does not outweigh convenience of Plaintiff's witnesses in a case concerning compliance with San Francisco Rent Ordinance, San Francisco property and San Francisco tenancy. Defendant's witnesses do not even claim such inconvenience. Defendant fails to establish prejudice of any one, let alone all judges, serving in San Francisco Superior Court. = (501/PHA - LATE TENTATIVE)

rulings.Case Title: GERTHERINE FORBES VS. FIRST MORTGAGE CORPORATION , et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff's Complaint With Prejudice DENIED. Dismissal was filed on February 26, 2014, which was a month before Notice of Entry of Order was served on Plaintiff. Defendant provides no admissible evidence that dismissal was not properly filed by Plaintiff's attorney of record. = (501/PHA) - LATE TENTATIVE
